title: Context Engineering and Repository Grounding · Contract Clinic: Context Refresh
summary: Ground an agent in a large unfamiliar repository without flooding its context. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt context refresh increment in a repository context map and retrieval plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Refresh drift-prone facts and summarize stable discoveries before context compaction.
Detect conflicting instructions and evidence and apply a documented precedence rule.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Context Refresh
steps.instruction:
theory case — Use a changed theory case for prompt context engineering that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

options.text: Once loaded, external documentation never needs revalidation. This does not resolve predict context refresh.

steps.title: Build Context Refresh evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-context-refresh-1ysjww" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Context Refresh into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-context-refresh-1ysjww
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Context Refresh model

content.title: Evidence defense
